subject: FW: fund summary
body:
________________________________

From: Anita Von Schoenborn [mailto:avs@nyc.rr.com] 
Sent: Monday, July 23, 2007 11:20 AM
To: Helen Kim
Subject: FW: fund summary

 

 

Dear Helen, 

I would appreciate it if you could forward this to Jeffrey

 

I spoke with Jeffrey several years ago and I am aware that he does not
usually invest in funds. However the other investors in this fund do not
typically invest in funds either and the minimum is $25 million, so
perhaps he might make an exception!

 

Pangaea One is a global special situations fund which will seek out
macro or micro dislocations and make minority control investments.
Typically family owned businesses but also corporate
distressed/divestment. The team is outstanding and has worked together
at AIG for nearly ten years. Their track record is top tier.

There will be no more than 20 investors in the fund. I have introduced
them to several of my clients, such as the Sainsbury family, a large
Swiss family and a prominent Middle Eastern family and they have all
committed.

U.S. investors include Sam Zell, the DeVoss family of Amway, John Malone
of Liberty Media, and Hank Greenberg, their former boss and head of AIG.
Institutional investors include the IMF pension fund and Dupont.

If Jeffrey has an interest, I would be glad to set up a conference call
with Peter Yu, the Founding Managing Partner. The final closing is
August 31st. 

 

 

Best, Anita
body_html:
From: Anita Von Schoenborn [mailto:avs@nyc.rr.com]
Sent: Monday, July 23, 2007 11:20 AM
To: Helen Kim
Subject: FW: fund summary

 

 

Dear Helen, 

I would appreciate it if you could forward this to Jeffrey

 

I spoke with Jeffrey several years ago and I am aware that he does not usually invest in funds. However the other investors in this fund do not typically invest in funds either and the minimum is $25 million, so perhaps he might make an exception!

 

Pangaea One is a global special situations fund which will seek out macro or micro dislocations and make minority control investments. Typically family owned businesses but also corporate distressed/divestment. The team is outstanding and has worked together at AIG for nearly ten years. Their track record is top tier.

There will be no more than 20 investors in the fund. I have introduced them to several of my clients, such as the Sainsbury family, a large Swiss family and a prominent Middle Eastern family and they have all committed.

U.S. investors include Sam Zell, the DeVoss family of Amway, John Malone of Liberty Media, and Hank Greenberg, their former boss and head of AIG. Institutional investors include the IMF pension fund and Dupont.

If Jeffrey has an interest, I would be glad to set up a conference call with Peter Yu, the Founding Managing Partner. The final closing is August 31st.

 

 

Best, Anita
